name: GT-orderunit-def
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:368-372 (1.2.1, definition of order-unit space and order norm), VERBATIM: "An element  \$e \in A^+\$  is called an order unit for A if for all  \$a \in A\$  there is  \$\lambda > 0\$  such that  \$-\lambda e \le a \le \lambda e\$ . We say A is Archimedean if  \$na \le e\$  for all  \$n \in \mathbb{N}\$ —the natural numbers—implies  \$a \le 0\$ . In this case A has a norm given by" ... "\$\$||a|| = \inf\{\lambda > 0: -\lambda e \le a \le \lambda e\}.\$\$" ... "A is said to be an order unit space if A has an order unit and A is Archimedean." This is the definition the conjunction (unit, order unit, Archimedean) must satisfy, and it fixes the order-norm formula ||a||=inf{t>0:-t e<=a<=t e}.
